text.title: Service Bootstrap Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the service-bootstrap-checklist methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Checklist contains exactly 12 items: repo, CI, logging, metrics, healthcheck, runbook, on-call rota, dashboards, alerts, secrets, deploy, postmortem template.
rule.rationale: Open-ended bootstrap lets services skip critical items; closed 12 is the empirically minimum floor.

rule.statement: Each item has a binary done criterion (URL/file/PR id). 'In progress' is not done.
rule.rationale: Soft completion lets services take traffic with half-wired observability.

rule.statement: Each item names a single owner (handle) accountable for upkeep, not creation.
rule.rationale: Owner-less items rot; named owners answer to drift.

rule.statement: A service does not take production traffic until all 12 items are done. Waivers require platform-lead approval recorded in the bootstrap record.
rule.rationale: Soft enforcement makes the floor decorative.

rule.statement: Dashboards must be in place before alerts are wired. Alerts without dashboards send on-call to blind investigation.
rule.rationale: Alert-before-dashboard is the most common cause of slow MTTR on new services.

rule.statement: Service has a postmortem template ready before traffic. First incident must not block on writing the template.
rule.rationale: Pre-loaded templates cut postmortem latency from days to hours.

rule.statement: If service is a spike OR template-inherited bootstrap OR internal cron with no external contract, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Bootstrap floor is wasted overhead in those configurations.
